text: The robotic sigmoidectomy procedure involves an extended lymph node dissection using the nose technique, targeting the tumor located in the middle part of the sigmoid colon.

The surgeon performs an extended lymph node dissection, starting with the skeletalization of the inferior mesenteric artery from its root. The first sigmoid artery is identified branching off the left colic artery, and it is clipped and divided at the root.

The surgeon clips and divides the second sigmoid artery branching from the inferior mesenteric artery. The mesocolonectomy begins with mobilization of the bowel from the medial side in the interfacial space, extending to the left lateral canal. The distal line of resection is prepared, and the bowel is closed with a clip.

The surgeon washes, staples, and divides the lumen of the bowel. The posterior vaginal vault is opened, and the anvil of the endostapler is delivered through the incision into the abdominal cavity. A colotomy is performed near the proximal line of resection, and the gut lumen is treated with an antiseptic solution.
The surgeon installs a sleeve into the vaginal orifice, locates its lumen, and extracts the specimen through the vaginal passage.

The surgeon inserts the base of the circular stapler into the rectal stump to perform a coloanal side-to-end anastomosis. The stapler is extracted, and the integrity of the rings is verified. A bubble test is performed, followed by the installation of drainage. The final view shows the abdominal wall.
